device_name: syngo.via MI Workflows; Scenium; syngo MBF
intended_use: syngo.via molecular imaging (MI) workflows comprise medical diagnostic applications for viewing, manipulation, quantification, analysis and comparison of medical images from single or multiple imaging
intended_use_full: syngo.via molecular imaging (MI) workflows comprise medical diagnostic applications for viewing, manipulation, quantification, analysis and comparison of medical images from single or multiple imaging modalities with one or more time-points. These workflows support functional data, such as positron emission tomography (PET) or nuclear medicine (NM), as well as anatomical datasets, such as computed tomography (CT) or magnetic resonance (MR). syngo.via MI workflows are intended to be utilized by appropriately trained health care professionals to aid in the management of diseases, including those associated with oncology, cardiology, neurology, and organ function.
target_condition: oncology, cardiology, neurology, and organ dysfunction; specifically includes support for tau and amyloid PET imaging for neurodegenerative diseases
ground_truth: Manually annotated ground truth for organ segmentation; MR-based segmentation and SUVR calculations for tau workflow validation; AAL atlas for Braak region definition
other_metrics: DICE coefficient (Organ Segmentation); Average Symmetric Surface Distance/ASSD (Organ Segmentation); SUVR correlation and agreement between tau workflow and MR-based method (Tau Workflow)
